heading: Good data visualisation principles
text: Match graph type data typeLabel axes clearly (variable names units)Use honest scales (usually starting zero)Avoid unnecessary decoration (especially 3D graphs)Aim tell truth communicate clearlyA graph good data displays. amount visual design can fix poor biased data.

heading: 1.2 Variables and Types of Data
text: Understanding data begins understanding variables.

heading: Statistical terminology
text: Cases: individuals objects studiedVariables: characteristics measured caseData: observed values variables

heading: Types of data
text: Categorical variables often coded numerically software, numbers numerical meaning.

heading: 1.3 Summarising and Displaying One Variable
text: distribution variable describes values takes often occur.

heading: Numerical summaries
text: Frequency tables categorical variablesFrequency distributions quantitative variables

heading: Bar charts
text: Used categorical variablesBars touchOrder bars logically (alphabetical size)

heading: Pie charts
text: recommended difficult reader fully understandShow parts wholeMust include categories (total = 100%)Best used sparingly categories

heading: 1.4 Contingency Tables (Two Categorical Variables)
text: analysing two categorical variables, use contingency table (also called two-way table cross-tabulation).

heading: Types of distributions
text: Joint distribution: proportions cases cellMarginal distribution: proportions one variable (row column totals)Conditional distribution: proportions within subgroup

heading: Assessing association
text: Two categorical variables associated conditional distributions differ groups. conditional distributions similar, variables considered independent.

heading: Graphical support
text: Stacked bar charts useful comparing conditional distributions visually.assess association, always compare conditional distributions, just raw counts.
